cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Tiffany could have gone to the market. First, let me list out all the known times Tiffany was somewhere else: - 6am: Tiffany woke up. - 6am to 9am: Tiffany was reading at the library. - 9am to 10am: Tiffany was driving to the water park. - 12pm to 6pm: Tiffany was buying a phone at the electronics store. - 6pm to 10pm: Tiffany was working out at the gym. - The market was closed after 10pm. Now, if I want to find when Tiffany could have gone to the market, I need to find the time periods when she wasn't doing anything else. Let's go through each period: From 6am to 9am, she was at the library. So she couldn't have gone to the market then. From 9am to 10am, she was driving to the water park. Again, not available. From 10am to 12pm, what was she doing? The last known activity was driving to the water park until 10am, and the next known activity is at 12pm at the electronics store. So between 10am and 12pm, there's no information about what Tiffany was doing. This is a possible window for her to go to the market. From 12pm to 6pm, she was at the electronics store buying a phone. From 6pm to 10pm, she was at the gym. After 10pm, the market was closed anyway. So the only time period when we know Tiffany wasn't doing anything else is from 10am to 12pm. This matches with option B. Let me double check.
